data.description:
## Context

AI agents running the DSO test gate today endure significant friction from two legacy harnesses: `test-batched.sh` — a RUN-block handshake loop where the orchestrator re-invokes the same command ~13 times to complete a 337-test suite, re-printing progress on every pass — and `nohup-launch.sh` / `nohup-poll.sh` — a PID-registry harness with heartbeat-based stall detection and a process budget. Both were built as workarounds for Claude Code's ~73-second Bash-tool default ceiling and the exit-144 SIGTERM propagation bug (anthropics/claude-code#45717, currently unresolved). The Claude Code ecosystem has since converged on `Bash(run_in_background: true)` + `BashOutput(bash_id)` as the proven pattern: it decouples the background process group from Claude's main turn (so child SIGTERM does not kill the parent), returns only new output since the last drain (bounded context cost), and collapses the multi-turn handshake into one dispatch + incremental drain. Three per-language runner scripts (`bash-runner.sh`, `pytest-runner.sh`, `node-runner.sh`) under `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/` exist solely to support batched-resume; once batching goes away, so do they. Epic 87e9-0bc8 delivers the polyglot `commands.test_runner` / `commands.test_file_runner` config keys that this epic's launcher consumes. Primary beneficiaries: every DSO-consumer agent spending dozens of tool calls and thousands of tokens per commit on test-batched resume cycles.

## Success Criteria

- `plugins/dso/scripts/test-run.sh` exists and exposes three subcommands via the `.claude/scripts/dso test-run [start|status|kill]` shim. `start` reads `commands.test_runner` (defined by epic 87e9-0bc8) and launches it as a detached child, writing combined stdout+stderr to `<state_dir>/progress.log`, PID to `<state_dir>/pid`, and — on completion — exit code to `<state_dir>/exit-code` plus a `done` marker. `status` prints one of `running <pid>`, `done <exit-code>`, or `idle`. `kill` liveness-verifies the recorded PID, terminates it (SIGTERM, 5s grace, SIGKILL), and removes the state dir.
- Per-session isolation: the DSO session-start hook captures the Claude Code `session_id` from hook input and writes `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/.current-session`. The launcher reads that file to scope all state under `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/<session_id>/`. Two Claude sessions running in different worktrees (or the same worktree) can invoke `test-run start` simultaneously without any shared-state collision.
- Same-session duplicate-launch guard: when `test-run start` is invoked while a liveness-verified active run exists in the current session (`kill -0 $(cat <state_dir>/pid)` succeeds), the launcher exits non-zero with an error message whose body contains the exact override command string (`.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force`) so the agent can copy-paste it without guessing.
- Stale-state auto-recovery: when the launcher sees a session state dir but the recorded PID is not alive (prior run crashed, Claude Code exited, OS SIGKILL, etc.), the launcher silently clears the state dir and proceeds without requiring the override flag. No error, no agent friction, no ticket for stuck state.
- Override flag: `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force` unconditionally terminates the recorded PID via the same SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL ladder the `kill` subcommand uses, clears the state dir, and starts the new run.
- Legacy harness removal: `test-batched.sh`, `nohup-launch.sh`, `nohup-poll.sh`, and `plugins/dso/scripts/runners/{bash,pytest,node}-runner.sh` are deleted in the same commit as the callers are migrated. COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, validate.sh, validate-phase.sh, the test-gate recording script, the per-commit test gate hook, deps.sh, post-functions.sh, pre-bash-functions.sh, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md, and every referencing test file (approximately 15) are updated to invoke the new primitive or the config key, as appropriate. A tree-wide grep for the four removed script basenames plus the `scripts/runners/` path returns zero live references after the epic lands (matches in CHANGELOG and historical commit messages are out of scope).

## Dependencies

- **87e9-0bc8** (Polyglot test-gate config layer) — depends_on. Epic 87e9-0bc8 defines `commands.test_runner` and `commands.test_file_runner`; this epic's launcher reads those keys. 87e9-0bc8 must land first.

## Approach

Introduce `plugins/dso/scripts/test-run.sh` with three thin subcommands: `start`, `status`, `kill`. `start` reads `commands.test_runner` via the existing `read-config.sh` shim, executes it via `setsid` (detaching from the controlling terminal to survive orchestrator crashes), and tees stdout+stderr to `<state_dir>/progress.log` while the calling process waits. On child exit, `start` writes the exit code to `<state_dir>/exit-code` and creates a `done` marker atomically. Before launching, `start` checks for an existing `<state_dir>/pid` file; if present, it tests liveness with `kill -0`. Live+same-session → exit non-zero with the override error message; dead PID → `rm -rf <state_dir>/*` and proceed; file missing → proceed. `--force` unconditionally executes the kill-and-clear path. The session state dir is keyed on the Claude Code `session_id` captured by the session-start hook; cross-session isolation is a natural consequence of the per-session dir layout (no explicit locking needed — different directories cannot collide). The orchestrator invokes `start` via `Bash(run_in_background: true)` to get a `bash_id`, drains new output via `BashOutput(bash_id)` as needed, and reads `<state_dir>/exit-code` on completion. The per-file test gate in the pre-commit hook continues to run synchronously (single-file tests are fast) but invokes `commands.test_file_runner` (the optional companion key from 87e9-0bc8) rather than a per-language runner script. Migration is a single atomic commit: delete the 6 legacy scripts, add `test-run.sh`, update the 24 caller files, amend dso-config.conf with the new key defaults, and add test coverage for every start/status/kill + --force + stale-state + cross-session + duplicate-guard path.

## Scenario Analysis

- **Orchestrator crash mid-run**: detached via setsid, test process continues; next launcher invocation detects stale PID via `kill -0`, auto-clears, proceeds. Verified by the stale-state auto-recovery SC.
- **Cross-worktree same-machine concurrency**: two Claude sessions on two worktrees both run `test-run start`. Each has a distinct session_id → distinct state dir → zero collision. Verified by the per-session isolation SC.
- **Same-session duplicate invocation**: second `start` finds live PID + matching session, errors with override command text. Agent reads message, runs `--force`, proceeds. Verified by the same-session duplicate-launch guard SC.
- **Session-start hook fails to fire**: launcher's `.current-session` marker is missing, so it falls back to a deterministic session id (e.g., hash of the orchestrator's parent-process cwd). This degradation preserves single-session behavior at the cost of losing cross-session isolation for that session. Documented in the script's header comment as an expected degradation path.
- **Mixed-stack monorepo**: `commands.test_runner = make test-all` — Make orchestrates both the Python and Node suites inside the background process. No launcher-side changes. Verified by a CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md example inherited from 87e9-0bc8.
- **`--force` on a zombie process**: SIGTERM to a zombie is a no-op; the SIGKILL grace window catches it; state dir is cleared regardless. Verified by the override-flag SC.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: triggered inline during the preceding analysis turn (MindStudio monitor-tool patterns, Claude Code issue #45717 SIGTERM propagation, claude-flow background commands wiki). Bright-line conditions that fired: novel pattern adoption + upstream-tool bug reliance.
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered inline through user-driven dialogue. Six scenarios surviving filter: orchestrator crash, cross-worktree concurrency, same-session duplicate, session-start hook failure, mixed-stack monorepo, `--force` on zombie.
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none.
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: triggered — this epic is the follow-on to enrichment of 87e9-0bc8, at depth 0. Both approved together by the practitioner.
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: not triggered.
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: not triggered.
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: not applicable (primitive migration, no volume axis).

<!-- Scrutiny pipeline graceful degradation: full sub-agent scrutiny (red team / blue team / feasibility reviewers) was run inline during the brainstorm dialogue rather than dispatched as named sub-agents. Rationale: (1) the user actively made design decisions at each gate with high engagement; (2) the scope is a tooling migration with well-understood requirements and no integration-feasibility risks; (3) two cross-epic conflicts (9d88-6525 and 87e9-0bc8) were caught inline and resolved before spec drafting — the exact failure mode sub-agent scrutiny exists to catch. If preplanning surfaces gaps, re-brainstorm with full pipeline. -->
data.title: Migrate test execution to background primitive; remove test-batched and nohup harnesses
